artifacts.description: Implementation plan: nix dry-run wrappers, mail-guard hook, mbsync freeze/thaw, aerc review querymap, notmuch postNew scaffolding, prior-art harvest/retire, and the two cross-repo handoffs

memory_candidates.content: Safety-envelope pattern for agent-driven destructive operations (confirmed-mutation-wrapper): (1) nix writeShellScriptBin wrappers that are dry-run by default, with mutation gated by a positive `--execute` flag plus `--confirm-manifest <sha256-of-approved-manifest-bytes>` recomputed and refused on mismatch; (2) execute mode diffs executed IDs against the human-approved manifest and never re-derives targets; (3) manifests keyed on a STABLE identifier (e.g., RFC Message-ID header), never a tool's ephemeral per-folder id, with a per-ID execution-status companion state file (pending|executed|failed) so the approved bytes stay immutable and re-runs are idempotent after a halt; (4) a PreToolUse Bash hook allowlisting only the wrapper binaries and denying the raw mutation commands, with allowlist/deny kept as isolated data arrays so the pattern is liftable; (5) two-layer enforcement: the hook gates only the agent's top-level Bash calls, the git-tracked wrapper source is layer 2. Reserve extensibility flags (e.g., --account) in the contract before multiple repos consume it. Breadcrumb from task 72 email infrastructure; do not build a generic framework until a second consumer exists.
